Source Resource Name: Mid- and later-life risk factors for predicting neuropathological brain changes associated with Alzheimer’s and vascular dementia: The Honolulu Asia Aging Study and the Age, Gene/Environment Susceptibility-Reykjavik Study
Data Repository: anonymized data available upon request to study management committee
Usage Description: Longitudinal population-based study that began in 1991 as an extension of the Honolulu Heart Program. The neuropathology sub-study included 852 autopsies obtained between April 1992 and August 2013. Models were developed for predicting Alzheimer's disease and non-AD neuropathologies using the HAAS neuropathological sub-study (n=852). Model accuracy for predicting clinical dementia over 30 years was tested in the non-autopsied HAAS sample (n=2960). The study included men of Japanese ancestry born between 1900 and 1919, with baseline exams from 1965-1968 (age range 45-68 years). Risk factors measured included demographics, cardio-metabolic health, blood and urine tests, lifestyle factors, and APOE e4 allele status. Neuropathological examination included assessment of amyloid plaques, neurofibrillary tangles, microvascular lesions, and lacunar infarcts.
Decision Rationale: neuropathology, Alzheimer's disease, dementia, autopsy, longitudinal cohort

Source Resource Name: Mid- and later-life risk factors for predicting neuropathological brain changes associated with Alzheimer’s and vascular dementia: The Honolulu Asia Aging Study and the Age, Gene/Environment Susceptibility-Reykjavik Study
Data Repository: anonymized data available upon request to study management committee
Usage Description: The AGES-Reykjavik study originates from the Reykjavik Study, which included men and women born in 1907-1935 and living in Reykjavik, Iceland in 1967. AGES-Reykjavik examinations (2002-2006) included 5764 of the surviving Reykjavik Study cohort. External validation of risk scores was undertaken in participants from AGES-Reykjavik (n=4614 after exclusions). The final analytical sample excludes persons with no dementia data (n=312) and persons with dementia other than AD and VaD (n=719). Dementia case ascertainment was based on a three-step procedure including Mini-Mental State Examination, Digit Symbol Substitution Test, diagnostic test battery, and neurologic examination. Risk factor information was collected in mid-life prior to dementia ascertainment. Follow-up ranged from 26 to 4499 days with 801 incident dementia cases including 492 diagnosed with AD and 20 with VaD.
Decision Rationale: dementia, Alzheimer's disease, vascular dementia, external validation, population-based cohort

Source Resource Name: Profiling people with Parkinson's disease at risk of cognitive decline: Insights from PPMI and ICICLE‐PD data
Usage Description: Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI) data were used, accessed on November 15, 2022. PPMI is an international, observational, cohort study with almost 50 sites across 13 countries. Eligibility was contingent on a diagnosis of PD no longer than 2 years prior to enrolment and drug naïve status. Participants with young-onset PD (age < 50) and dementia were excluded. Data were available annually from baseline up to year 7. Participants with two or more data points were included, resulting in N= 770 baseline participants. Latent class mixed models were created to identify subgroups with similar longitudinal cognitive trajectories using total MoCA score as the outcome variable.
Decision Rationale: Parkinson's disease, cognitive decline, longitudinal cohort

Source Resource Name: Profiling people with Parkinson's disease at risk of cognitive decline: Insights from PPMI and ICICLE‐PD data
Usage Description: Incidence of Cognitive Impairment in Cohorts with Longitudinal Evaluation-Parkinson's Disease (ICICLE-PD) data were used, accessed on October 10, 2022. ICICLE-PD was conducted in accordance with the Declaration of Helsinki and GCP guidelines. Ethical approval was provided by the Newcastle and North Tyneside Research Ethics Committee. Recruitment took place from the community in Cambridgeshire and Newcastle upon Tyne/Gateshead, United Kingdom. All participants were recently diagnosed people with PD meeting Queen's Square Brain Bank Criteria, with working knowledge of English and no significant baseline cognitive impairment. Data were available every 18-months from baseline to year 4.5. Participants with two or more data points were included, resulting in N= 212 baseline participants. Latent class mixed models were applied to identify cognitive subtypes based on rate of decline in MoCA scores.
Decision Rationale: Parkinson's disease, cognitive impairment, community-based cohort

Source Resource Name: Early-stage idiopathic Parkinson’s disease is associated with reduced circular RNA expression
Usage Description: The Michael J Fox Foundation Parkinson's Progression Markers Initiative (PPMI) discovery cohort used for RNA sequencing of whole blood in early-stage idiopathic PD patients. PPMI = 259 PD, 161 Controls. Whole blood total RNA sequencing was performed to detect and quantify linear and circular RNAs. Participants were not receiving treatment at sample collection.
Decision Rationale: Parkinson's disease, whole blood RNA sequencing, circular RNA

Source Resource Name: Early-stage idiopathic Parkinson’s disease is associated with reduced circular RNA expression
Usage Description: The Incidence of Cognitive Impairment in Cohorts with Longitudinal Evaluation-PD (ICICLE-PD) replication cohort used for RNA sequencing validation. ICICLE-PD = 48 PD, 48 Controls. Whole blood total RNA sequencing was performed to detect and quantify linear and circular RNAs. Most ICICLE-PD participants were receiving dopaminergic treatment at sample collection.
Decision Rationale: Parkinson's disease, whole blood RNA sequencing, early-stage diagnosis
